sourceMovie.title: Glass
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Glass works as a thriller movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The thriller and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: No Escape
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: No Escape (1994) belongs to the same conversation as Glass — both anchor their thriller in drama emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Iron Giant
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Glass's drama stayed with you, The Iron Giant (1999) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The science-fiction through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Promising Young Woman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Glass excels in thriller, Promising Young Woman (2020) doubles down on drama to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Spectral
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Spectral (2016) is the natural next step after Glass: same thriller DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and science-fiction elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Glass who respond to its science-fiction will find Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 (2017) immediately familiar. Both films share a adventure sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Glass fans keep landing on Spider-Man: No Way Home (2021) — the science-fiction is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Split
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Split draws from the same thriller well as Glass, channeling horror in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2017 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Spider-Man 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Spider-Man 2 (2004) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Glass later expanded. If Glass resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction appeal of Glass — that tense edge, the action — translates cleanly into The Amazing Spider-Man 2 (2014). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Incredible Hulk
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Incredible Hulk (2008) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps Glass rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

faq.question: What should I watch after Glass?
faq.answer: Try No Escape, The Iron Giant, Promising Young Woman—each shares DNA with Glass while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Glass?
faq.answer: Glass (2019) is best described as Thriller, Drama, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Glass worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
